title: Profile setup vs create user profile vs onboarding vs account creation
summary: Use profile setup for completing or improving an existing profile, create user profile for first visible identity creation, onboarding for first-use activation, and account creation for persistent credentials and recovery.
decisionRules: Choose profile setup when the user already has a usable account or profile and is guided through missing, stale, recommended, managed, or visibility-sensitive profile fields.
Choose create user profile when the primary task is first establishing the visible identity that will appear in mentions, directories, comments, assignments, public pages, or profile cards.
Choose onboarding when the work is teaching the product, selecting a first goal, creating sample content, or reaching first value rather than improving profile completeness.
Choose account creation when the user is establishing persistent access, credentials, verification, recovery, authorization, or save-and-return behavior.
Choose inline edit when the user changes one profile value in place and does not need a guided setup checklist, profile preview, visibility review, or return-later state.
Choose review before submit when profile changes are public, regulated, high-risk, or hard to undo and need a final pre-commit review gate.
Profile setup should separate required access fields from recommended enrichment fields; optional photo, bio, location, pronouns, phone, and social links should not block unrelated work.
Profile setup should show where each field appears, including team cards, directories, mentions, public pages, marketplace listings, search, exports, and app access.
Managed or synced fields belong in profile setup only as read-only values with source and correction route, not editable fields that will be silently overwritten.
A profile setup completion meter is appropriate only when it reflects user-beneficial readiness and honors intentional skips; it should not pressure disclosure of unnecessary personal data.
failureModes: A product labels optional enrichment as required profile completion and blocks the user's real task.
A profile setup prompt asks for public location, phone number, or birthday without explaining visibility or audience.
Users edit a synced job title during profile setup and the value later reverts without explanation.
Profile setup is buried inside onboarding, so users cannot skip teaching without also losing profile draft progress.
Account creation asks for profile photo, bio, social links, and marketing preferences before users can establish credentials.
A profile setup preview does not match the actual directory row, comment byline, public profile, or app-visible profile.

sources.note: Atlassian supports field visibility, public-name behavior, photo fallback, and warnings about where profile information appears.
Slack supports collaboration profile fields, searchable fields, custom fields, identity-provider syncing, and admin management.
GitHub supports public profile customization, user control over included information, pinned items, profile README, status, and visibility options.
Slack supports full-name and display-name distinctions, mention behavior, and single sign-on restrictions.
NN/g supports clear required-field marking when profile setup mixes required and optional fields.
